text:
# Cross-Model Check — xmodel-diff.patch

**Reviewer:** codex
**Primary author/model:** claude
**Mode:** code
**Date:** 2026-05-29
**Verdict:** Challenge
**Status:** ok

---

I’ll check the surrounding zergboard skin/button code so the review is grounded in actual usage, not just the pasted diff.
The core change is small, but I’m checking persistence and the compatibility CSS overrides because skin state is hydrated in two paths. That’s where drift usually shows up in this kind of patch.
**Verdict:** Challenge

**HIGH findings (3):**
- `assets/css/main.css:75` changes `.hover:bg-lime-950:hover` text to `--skin-button-fg`, but `assets/css/main.css:98` later changes that hover background back to `--skin-panel`, yielding black text on a dark panel on hover.
- `assets/css/main.css:535` leaves `.zb-tab.is-active` as `--skin-accent-contrast` on solid `#6FBE31`, so active tabs now have low-contrast pale text on bright green.
- Several other solid-accent states still use `--skin-accent-contrast` after the accent was brightened, e.g. `AppSidebar.vue:925`, `BoardActivityDrawer.vue:152`, and `main.css:380`.
## MEDIUM
- `pages/app/settings.vue` and `pages/app/integrations/github.vue` do not include `buttonFg` in `PaletteKey`, `paletteDraft`, or `setCustomPalette`, so custom skins cannot preserve/control the new foreground token.
- `nuxt.config.ts` still has a separate prepaint preset copy missing `graphite` and `paper`, so those stored skins flash/fallback to terminal before hydration.

## LOW
- The comment in `pages/index.vue` references `~/Downloads/zergboard-landing-audit-2026-05-27.md`, which is a private local artifact and will not help future maintainers.
- The CSS comments are longer than the surrounding token-style file comments and add rationale that probably belongs in the audit/PR, not every runtime stylesheet load.

## Likely-missed-by-primary
- The duplicate `.hover:bg-lime-950:hover` selectors are order-dependent: the later rule only overwrites background, not the newly changed foreground.
- The fix treats `zb-button` as the only solid accent surface, but the app already uses solid accent for tabs, sidebar active items, activity filters, drag/status UI, and badges.
- `buttonFg` was added to the central skin type but not to the appearance editors, creating a partial schema migration for persisted custom palettes.

## Notes
- I reviewed the surrounding code paths only; no test or browser run was performed.
